name: Sant Joan Samora and Can Rimundet Waterfall Loop
name_original: Cascada de Sant Joan Samora - circular desde Martorell
description: This moderate 17.9-mile touring cycling route from Martorell takes you to the Cascada de Sant Joan Samora, featuring varied terrain and vine
region_intro: Hiking around Martorell offers diverse landscapes, situated along the Llobregat River and within the Catalan pre-coastal mountain range. The region features pine forests, unique red sandstone formations, and accessible river paths. Its proximity to Montserrat Mountain provides additional opportunities for varied terrain, from gentle riverside walks to more challenging mountainous ascents. Best hiking trails around Martorell The most popular hiking route is Castles of Rocafort and Rosanes Loop, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes medieval castles and unique red sandstone formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Turó del Castell and Turó de les Deveses Loop, a difficult 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This trail involves significant elevation gain, rewarding hikers with panoramic views and castle ruins. Local hikers also love the Llobregat Trail, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail leading through the Llobregat River valley, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Hiking around Martorell is defined by river paths along the Llobregat, ascents in the pre-coastal mountains, and distinctive red sandstone formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to difficult climbs. The routes in Martorell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 230 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Martorell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Martorell?
A: Martorell offers a diverse range of hiking experiences. You'll find accessible river paths along the Llobregat, ascents into the Catalan pre-coastal mountains, and routes featuring unique red sandstone formations. The area provides options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Martorell?
A: Yes, Martorell has several easy routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40383122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Landscape loop from Martorell - Enllaç&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.9 km path. The region offers a total of 6 easy routes, many of which are scenic loops or river paths.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Martorell?
A: Absolutely. Many of Martorell's trails are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Notable circular routes include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358403926/castles-of-rocafort-and-rosanes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castles of Rocafort and Rosanes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past medieval castles, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358411649/turo-del-castell-and-turo-de-les-deveses-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó del Castell and Turó de les Deveses Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: What kind of landmarks or viewpoints can I expect on Martorell's trails?
A: Hikers can discover a mix of historical and natural landmarks. Trails often pass medieval castles, such as those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358403926/castles-of-rocafort-and-rosanes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castles of Rocafort and Rosanes Loop&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also encounter unique red sandstone formations and enjoy panoramic views from higher points like the Turó del Castell or the Creu d'Aragall Pass. The historic Pont del Diable (Devil's Bridge) is another significant sight along the Llobregat River.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges to explore near Martorell?
A: Yes, the area around Martorell features several beautiful waterfalls and gorges. You can visit highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1423555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Mola&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1411041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1558506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Rimundet's Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These natural attractions offer refreshing stops and scenic views during your hike.

Q: Is Martorell a good base for exploring Montserrat Mountain?
A: Martorell is strategically located as a gateway to the iconic Montserrat Mountain. While some routes from Martorell to Montserrat can be quite long, its proximity makes it an excellent starting point for day trips to explore Montserrat Natural Park's diverse trails, from easy walks to the challenging Sant Jeroni Summit.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Martorell?
A: The hiking routes in Martorell are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 230 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from river paths to mountain ascents, and the distinctive natural features like the red sandstone formations and historical castles.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Martorell?
A: Martorell offers several routes suitable for families. The easier scenic loops and sections of the Llobregat Trail provide accessible paths with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed outing with children. The diverse landscapes ensure there's always something interesting to see.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Martorell?
A: Given its Mediterranean mountainous environment with pine forests and bushes, Martorell is generally suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while winter can also be enjoyable with cooler, crisp air. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during that season.

Q: How long do hikes around Martorell typically take?
A: Hikes in Martorell vary in length and duration. You can find shorter, easy loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40383122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Landscape loop from Martorell - Enllaç&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 1 hour 30 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358411649/turo-del-castell-and-turo-de-les-deveses-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó del Castell and Turó de les Deveses Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 3 hours 50 minutes to complete.

Q: Are the trails around Martorell accessible by public transport or do they have parking?
A: Martorell is a municipality with good accessibility. The Llobregat River trail, for instance, is known for its accessibility. While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, many routes start from or near the town center, which typically offers parking options. Public transport connections to Martorell itself are also available, making it possible to reach trail starting points.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cascada de Sant Joan Samora circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of paved and unpaved sections. While some parts are suitable for road bikes, other segments, particularly the path leading to the waterfall, transition to dirt tracks. This varied terrain means a hybrid, gravel, or even a mountain bike might be more comfortable for touring cyclists, depending on your preference and skill level.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' and requires good fitness due to its distance (28.8 km) and elevation gain (450 meters). While not extremely difficult, it's not a leisurely ride and includes some climbs and varied terrain, making it less ideal for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Cascada de Sant Joan Samora circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this touring bicycle route is approximately 2 hours and 28 minutes, assuming a steady pace. This can vary based on your fitness level, the type of bike you're using, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cascada de Sant Joan Samora circular route in Martorell?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Martorell, you can look for public parking options within the town. Specific trailhead parking is not detailed, but Martorell offers various parking facilities suitable for starting your cycling tour.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight of this route is the Can Rimundet's Waterfall (Cascada de Sant Joan de Samora). You'll also cycle through rural landscapes characterized by vineyards and fields, offering a tranquil experience away from urban centers. Another notable point of interest is the Sant Joan Samora Romanesque Church.

faqs.question: Is the Cascada de Sant Joan Samora circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs on this particular cycling route are not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with varied terrain or near roads, and to ensure they are comfortable with longer cycling distances.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Cascada de Sant Joan Samora circular route. It's an open public route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Catalonia generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn, with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and potentially more rain. Check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route or in Martorell?
faqs.answer: Martorell, as the starting and ending point, offers various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel before or after your ride. The route itself passes through rural areas, so it's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: Is it safe to swim in the Cascada de Sant Joan Samora waterfall?
faqs.answer: No, the water at Cascada de Sant Joan de Samora is not suitable for bathing due to potential contamination. It's best to enjoy the waterfall's beauty from a distance.

faqs.question: How can I reach the trailhead by public transport from Barcelona?
faqs.answer: Martorell is well-connected by public transport from Barcelona. You can typically take a train (e.g., FGC or Rodalies) from Barcelona to Martorell. From the Martorell station, you can then cycle to the starting point of the circular route.
